pod.name: anima-p4-alm-r8c-fp8
pod.dedicated_to: FP8 only — torch 2.9 pin, no Unsloth, no r8a/b rotation

install_results.prior_agent_state: torch 2.9.0+cu126 and transformer_engine 2.13.0 were ALREADY installed by predecessor agent before its API 500 death. No reinstall needed.
install_results.libcudart_mismatch_discovered: Image CUDA 12.4 libcudart loaded via ldconfig; torch cu126 wheels need libcudart 12.6+ which IS bundled in pip nvidia-cuda-runtime-cu12==12.6.77 but not in LD path by default. Symbol cudaGetDriverEntryPointByVersion missing from libcudart.so.12.4.127.
install_results.fix: Prepend LD_LIBRARY_PATH with /usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/nvidia/{cuda_runtime,cudnn,cublas,cusparse,cusolver,cufft,curand,nccl,nvjitlink}/lib — works without reinstall
install_results.te_smoke_verdict: PASS — fp8_autocast + te.Linear forward+backward work on H100 SM_90 (cap 9,0)

smoke_20step_results.prior_agent_error: launch script passed --round 8c (string) to int parser — fixed: --round 8
smoke_20step_results.loss_verdict: CRITICAL ANOMALY — loss ~15 is near random-init CE for vocab 152k. r6/r7 baseline loss at step 20 = ~2-3. Delta of ~13 CE points means base weights are effectively randomly initialized.
smoke_20step_results.root_cause_loss_anomaly: nn.Linear -> te.Linear monkey-patch runs BEFORE AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained. TE's te.Linear expects _extra_state entries in checkpoint which Qwen2.5-14B safetensors do NOT contain. HF warning confirms: 'newly initialized: [lm_head._extra_state, model.layers.*.mlp.{down,gate,up}_proj._extra_state, model.layers.*.self_attn.{k,o,q,v}_proj._extra_state]' — ALL 48 layers' attn+MLP projections are NEWLY INITIALIZED. The model's pretrained weights were NOT loaded. This is a fundamental incompatibility between TE's weight layout and HF's safetensors format for non-TE-native models.
smoke_20step_results.accuracy_check: FAIL — CE at step 20 = 15.2487, far above bf16 baseline ~2.5 range. Training a random-init model, not fine-tuning.
smoke_20step_results.nan_inf_check: PASS — no NaN/Inf, numerics stable, but on a random-init model

speed_measurement.decision: SKIPPED 50-step extended measurement — 20-step smoke already gave steady-state number and correctness FAIL means extended measurement would waste budget.
speed_measurement.flops_per_step_note: 6 * 14e9 params * 8192 tokens * 1.33 (grad ckpt 4x fwd adjusted to 3x effective for LoRA) = 9.17e14
speed_measurement.speed_verdict: FP8 path is SLOWER than bf16 baseline by ~57% (vs r7) and ~47% (vs r8a). Root causes: (1) torch.compile forced to mode=default when fp8_active, disabling cudagraphs+max-autotune (r7 didn't have compile either, so this isn't the delta), (2) TE custom autograd path has per-op overhead without compiler fusion, (3) gradient checkpointing ON forces 4x forward recompute which nullifies FP8 forward savings, (4) TE amax history sync every step adds allreduce-like latency, (5) the model is loaded as te.Linear but LoRA adapters on top are bf16 nn.Linear — every forward crosses dtype boundaries triggering implicit casts.

verdict_detail: r8c FP8 measurement is INVALID for apples-to-apples with r7. (1) Loss ~15 proves the base model is random-init due to TE monkey-patch breaking HF weight load — this is a correctness failure, not just accuracy degradation. (2) Even accepting the broken stack, speed is 33.8% bf16-eq MFU, far below r7's 62.6%, r8a's 48.12%, and the 75% bf16 breakthrough threshold. (3) 16.9% FP8-peak MFU is below the 40% threshold. Neither celebration condition met.

what_worked: torch 2.9 + TE 2.13 install on runpod/pytorch:2.4.0 image (with LD_LIBRARY_PATH fix)
TE fp8_autocast forward+backward on H100 SM_90 (verified via standalone te.Linear test)
Trainer patch applied correctly, import guard works, --fp8 flag gates properly
Pod provisioning flow (previous agent) and 20-step smoke launch
what_failed: The fundamental FP8+HF integration strategy — monkey-patching nn.Linear to te.Linear BEFORE from_pretrained is incompatible with safetensors that don't contain _extra_state entries. Patch disclaimer #1 warned about this but the patch implementation still used this approach. The patch would need to either: (a) wrap individual modules post-load via module replacement with weight copy, or (b) use accelerate's FP8RecipeKwargs which has a different integration mechanism, or (c) wait for HF+TE native integration (not available as of 2026-04-15).

Even if correctness were fixed, speed uplift is negative — FP8 via post-load wrapper would still suffer from gradient checkpointing 4x fwd recompute nullifying FP8 benefit for LoRA where compute is dominated by base-model forward.
lessons_for_future_fp8_attempts: FP8 on HuggingFace Qwen2 requires accelerate.Accelerator(fp8_recipe_handler=...) path, NOT raw TE monkey-patch
FP8 + gradient checkpointing is ANTI-SYNERGISTIC — grad ckpt recomputes FP8 forward which has higher fixed overhead per call than bf16
FP8 benefit materializes mainly with: no grad ckpt + torch.compile max-autotune + NeMo-native architecture + batch size >= 4 (compute-bound)
For 14B LoRA fine-tune, the fastest path is the r7/r8a Unsloth stack (bf16 + fused kernels + tf32 + cudnn), NOT FP8 retrofit
honest_analysis.recommendation: ABANDON FP8 retrofit for current HF-based trainer. Route FP8 exploration via (1) accelerate Accelerator FP8 path, OR (2) full rewrite as NeMo/Megatron-LM native training. Neither is P4 scope. For r8 ceiling breakthrough, pursue: batch sweep (#26), nn_core Tensor port (#27), or r8a Unsloth speedups (#28).

cost_accounting.wasted_vs_informed: informed — clean FAIL verdict with detailed root cause unblocks #26/#27/#28 for true ceiling push

r7_baseline_for_comparison.note: Fair comparison against same b/g/s config
